condition_specific: bladder cancer, brain cancer, colon cancer, non-Hodgkin's lymphoma, kidney cancer, liver cancer, lymphatic or haematopoietic cancer, prostate cancer, testicular cancer, skin cancer, cervical cancer, breast cancer
filing_deadline_notes: Cancer must develop and cause death within 10 years of separation from service
notes: CRITICAL: This provision creates a line-of-duty DEATH BENEFIT presumption only — the statute explicitly states it does NOT create a WC presumption under KRS Chapter 342 and shall not be construed to do so. Conditions: firefighter for at least 5 consecutive years; cancer-related death within 10 years of separation; no tobacco use for 10 years prior to diagnosis; under age 65 at time of death; no pre-employment cancer diagnosis; exposed to IARC or NTP recognized carcinogen reasonably associated with the cancer. Rebuttability not explicitly stated for this benefit.

condition_specific: post-traumatic stress injury / post-traumatic stress disorder
notes: Covers full-time, career, and volunteer firefighters. Diagnosis must be by psychiatrist, psychologist, or licensed professional counselor (KRS 335.500–335.599). PTSD must be caused by an event or accumulation of events in course and scope of employment. NOTE: This provision is a mental health treatment reimbursement program (out-of-pocket costs reimbursed by commission), not a traditional WC compensability presumption — maximum 12 months of reimbursement. Personnel action exclusion applies (transfer, promotion, termination not compensable).

condition_specific: post-traumatic stress injury / post-traumatic stress disorder
notes: Explicitly covers volunteer firefighters. Same diagnosis and reimbursement structure as career firefighters. Mental health treatment reimbursement program, not WC compensability presumption. Maximum 12 months reimbursement.
